steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The underlined claim states that 'Aha Pūnana Leo increased the number of young people who speak Hawaiian. To support it, we need direct evidence of growth in young Hawaiian speakers that is specifically tied to that school.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Pacific Islander populations in Hawai'i': Describes Samoan residents, not Hawaiian-speaking youth. No connection to the school or language growth. ✗
  • Choice B — 'fewer than fifty children ... now more than 2,000 students': Directly shows the before-and-after count of young Hawaiian speakers at the school, proving the claim of increase. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Hawaiian is similar to Tahitian, Samoan, and Maori': Discusses linguistic relationships, not speaker numbers or the school's impact. ✗
  • Choice D — 'roughly 680,000 Native Hawaiians in the US': A population statistic with no information about language ability or young speakers. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
